TEXT.citation: Wikipedia contributors. (2026). Sakizaya Wikipedia. Wikimedia Foundation. Retrieved 2026-06-09, from https://szy.wikipedia.org/
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy, title = {Sakizaya Wikipedia}, author = {{Wikipedia contributors}}, year = {2026}, publisher = {Wikimedia Foundation}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/}, urldate = {2026-06-09} }

S.FORM: == balaysan == È´òÁ≤± balaysan ku sapisanga' tu epah. siwkay nu balaysan u balaysan sa, u nipaluma nu niyazu'. cacay a mihca tabakiay a lutuk. hali caledes, caay katalaw tu caledes,caay katalaw tu balad. kya paenu' sa kya kulit u sumanahay atu sangelacay. mukan ku tademaw, mukan tu ku aadupan. taneng misanga' tu epah. u kapahay a nipaluma nu niyazu'. u balaysan a papah siwalak, sisa amana mukan kya papah, amana pakan tu aadupan. izaw ku kungku nu Sakizaya, izaw ku sibalaysan a subal, u tatayna amin, inayi' ku tatama. tinaku a kamu mibalaysan Êé°È´òÁ≤± mibalaysan ku malukay tu pituway a bulad nu miheca. mibalaysanay Êé°È´òÊ¢ÅÁöÑ tanutuwa' sa mudaliw ku mibalaysanay a tademaw. sibalaysan ÊúâÈ´òÁ≤± sibalaysan ku ni palumaan ni Tubah a kubkub. sibalaysanay ÊúâÈ´òÁ≤±ÁöÑ nayi' tu ku sibalaysanay nu malukay ayza. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika Sorghum bicolor, commonly called sorghum (/'s…îÀêr…°…ôm/) and also known as great millet, durra, jowari, or milo, is a grass species cultivated for its grain, which is used for food, both for animals and humans, and for ethanol production. Sorghum originated in northern Africa, and is now cultivated widely in tropical and subtropical regions. Sorghum is the world's fifth most important cereal crop after rice, wheat, maize and barley. S. bicolor is typically an annual, but some cultivars are perennial. It grows in clumps that may reach over 4 m high. The grain is small, ranging from 2 to 4 mm in diameter. Sweet sorghums are sorghum cultivars that are primarily grown for foliage, syrup production, and ethanol; they are taller than those grown for grain.
S.PHON: == bar~…æajsan == ** bar~…æajsan ku sapisa≈ãa î~ ° tu …ôpaƒß. siwkaj nu bar~…æajsan u bar~…æajsan sa, u nipar~…æuma nu nijazu î~ °. ¶a ¶aj a miƒß ¶a tabakiaj a r~…æutuk. ƒßar~…æi ¶ar~…æ…ôd…ôs, ¶aaj katar~…æaw tu ¶ar~…æ…ôd…ôs, ¶aaj katar~…æaw tu bar~…æad. kja pa…ônu î~ ° sa kja kur~…æit u sumanaƒßaj atu sa≈ã…ôr~…æa ¶aj. mukan ku tad…ômaw, mukan tu ku aadupan. tan…ô≈ã misa≈ãa î~ ° tu …ôpaƒß. u kapaƒßaj a nipar~…æuma nu nijazu î~ °. u bar~…æajsan a papaƒß siwar~…æak, sisa amana mukan kja papaƒß, amana pakan tu aadupan. izaw ku ku≈ãku nu sakizaja, izaw ku sibar~…æajsan a subar~…æ, u tatajna amin, inaji î~ ° ku tatama. tinaku a kamu mibar~…æajsan *** mibar~…æajsan ku mar~…æukaj tu pituwaj a bur~…æad nu miƒß…ô ¶a. mibar~…æajsanaj **** tanutuwa î~ ° sa mudar~…æiw ku mibar~…æajsanaj a tad…ômaw. sibar~…æajsan *** sibar~…æajsan ku ni par~…æumaan ni tubaƒß a kubkub. sibar~…æajsanaj **** naji î~ ° tu ku sibar~…æajsanaj nu mar~…æukaj ajza. u sur~…æit nu ƒßur~…æam atu amir~…æika sorgƒßum bi ¶or~…æor, ¶ommonr~…æj ¶ar~…ær~…æ…ôd sorgƒßum (/ î~ °s**r*…ôm/) and ar~…æso known as gr…ôat mir~…ær~…æ…ôt, durra, jowari, or mir~…æo, is a grass sp…ô ¶i…ôs ¶ur~…æti*at…ôd for its grain, wƒßi ¶ƒß is us…ôd for food, botƒß for animar~…æs and ƒßumans, and for …ôtƒßanor~…æ produ ¶tion. sorgƒßum originat…ôd in nortƒß…ôrn afri ¶a, and is now ¶ur~…æti*at…ôd wid…ôr~…æj in tropi ¶ar~…æ and subtropi ¶ar~…æ r…ôgions. sorgƒßum is tƒß…ô worr~…æd î~ °s fiftƒß most important ¶…ôr…ôar~…æ ¶rop aft…ôr ri ¶…ô, wƒß…ôat, maiz…ô and barr~…æ…ô. s. bi ¶or~…æor is tjpi ¶ar~…ær~…æj an annuar~…æ, but som…ô ¶ur~…æti*ars ar…ô p…ôr…ônniar~…æ. it grows in ¶r~…æumps tƒßat maj r…ôa ¶ƒß o*…ôr * m ƒßigƒß. tƒß…ô grain is smar~…ær~…æ, ra≈ãi≈ã from * to * mm in diam…ôt…ôr. sw…ô…ôt sorgƒßums ar…ô sorgƒßum ¶ur~…æti*ars tƒßat ar…ô primarir~…æj grown for for~…æiag…ô, sjrup produ ¶tion, and …ôtƒßanor~…æ; tƒß…ô ar…ô tar~…ær~…æ…ôr tƒßan tƒßos…ô grown for grain.
S.FORM: == balaysan == È´òÁ≤± balaysan ku sapisanga' tu epah. siwkay nu balaysan u balaysan sa, u nipaluma nu niyazu'. cacay a mihca tabakiay a lutuk. hali caledes, caay katalaw tu caledes,caay katalaw tu balad. kya paenu' sa kya kulit u sumanahay atu sangelacay. mukan ku tademaw, mukan tu ku aadupan. taneng misanga' tu epah. u kapahay a nipaluma nu niyazu'. u balaysan a papah siwalak, sisa amana mukan kya papah, amana pakan tu aadupan. izaw ku kungku nu Sakizaya, izaw ku sibalaysan a subal, u tatayna amin, inayi' ku tatama. tinaku a kamu mibalaysan Êé°È´òÁ≤± mibalaysan ku malukay tu pituway a bulad nu miheca. mibalaysanay Êé°È´òÊ¢ÅÁöÑ tanutuwa' sa mudaliw ku mibalaysanay a tademaw. sibalaysan ÊúâÈ´òÁ≤± sibalaysan ku ni palumaan ni Tubah a kubkub. sibalaysanay ÊúâÈ´òÁ≤±ÁöÑ nayi' tu ku sibalaysanay nu malukay ayza. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika Sorghum bicolor, commonly called sorghum (/'s…îÀêr…°…ôm/) and also known as great millet, durra, jowari, or milo, is a grass species cultivated for its grain, which is used for food, both for animals and humans, and for ethanol production. Sorghum originated in northern Africa, and is now cultivated widely in tropical and subtropical regions. Sorghum is the world's fifth most important cereal crop after rice, wheat, maize and barley. S. bicolor is typically an annual, but some cultivars are perennial. It grows in clumps that may reach over 4 m high. The grain is small, ranging from 2 to 4 mm in diameter. Sweet sorghums are sorghum cultivars that are primarily grown for foliage, syrup production, and ethanol; they are taller than those grown for grain.
S.PHON: == bar~…æajsan == ** bar~…æajsan ku sapisa≈ãa î~ ° tu …ôpaƒß. siwkaj nu bar~…æajsan u bar~…æajsan sa, u nipar~…æuma nu nijazu î~ °. ¶a ¶aj a miƒß ¶a tabakiaj a r~…æutuk. ƒßar~…æi ¶ar~…æ…ôd…ôs, ¶aaj katar~…æaw tu ¶ar~…æ…ôd…ôs, ¶aaj katar~…æaw tu bar~…æad. kja pa…ônu î~ ° sa kja kur~…æit u sumanaƒßaj atu sa≈ã…ôr~…æa ¶aj. mukan ku tad…ômaw, mukan tu ku aadupan. tan…ô≈ã misa≈ãa î~ ° tu …ôpaƒß. u kapaƒßaj a nipar~…æuma nu nijazu î~ °. u bar~…æajsan a papaƒß siwar~…æak, sisa amana mukan kja papaƒß, amana pakan tu aadupan. izaw ku ku≈ãku nu sakizaja, izaw ku sibar~…æajsan a subar~…æ, u tatajna amin, inaji î~ ° ku tatama. tinaku a kamu mibar~…æajsan *** mibar~…æajsan ku mar~…æukaj tu pituwaj a bur~…æad nu miƒß…ô ¶a. mibar~…æajsanaj **** tanutuwa î~ ° sa mudar~…æiw ku mibar~…æajsanaj a tad…ômaw. sibar~…æajsan *** sibar~…æajsan ku ni par~…æumaan ni tubaƒß a kubkub. sibar~…æajsanaj **** naji î~ ° tu ku sibar~…æajsanaj nu mar~…æukaj ajza. u sur~…æit nu ƒßur~…æam atu amir~…æika sorgƒßum bi ¶or~…æor, ¶ommonr~…æj ¶ar~…ær~…æ…ôd sorgƒßum (/ î~ °s**r*…ôm/) and ar~…æso known as gr…ôat mir~…ær~…æ…ôt, durra, jowari, or mir~…æo, is a grass sp…ô ¶i…ôs ¶ur~…æti*at…ôd for its grain, wƒßi ¶ƒß is us…ôd for food, botƒß for animar~…æs and ƒßumans, and for …ôtƒßanor~…æ produ ¶tion. sorgƒßum originat…ôd in nortƒß…ôrn afri ¶a, and is now ¶ur~…æti*at…ôd wid…ôr~…æj in tropi ¶ar~…æ and subtropi ¶ar~…æ r…ôgions. sorgƒßum is tƒß…ô worr~…æd î~ °s fiftƒß most important ¶…ôr…ôar~…æ ¶rop aft…ôr ri ¶…ô, wƒß…ôat, maiz…ô and barr~…æ…ô. s. bi ¶or~…æor is tjpi ¶ar~…ær~…æj an annuar~…æ, but som…ô ¶ur~…æti*ars ar…ô p…ôr…ônniar~…æ. it grows in ¶r~…æumps tƒßat maj r…ôa ¶ƒß o*…ôr * m ƒßigƒß. tƒß…ô grain is smar~…ær~…æ, ra≈ãi≈ã from * to * mm in diam…ôt…ôr. sw…ô…ôt sorgƒßums ar…ô sorgƒßum ¶ur~…æti*ars tƒßat ar…ô primarir~…æj grown for for~…æiag…ô, sjrup produ ¶tion, and …ôtƒßanor~…æ; tƒß…ô ar…ô tar~…ær~…æ…ôr tƒßan tƒßos…ô grown for grain.
